description: Adapter pattern and repo introspection agent for the OSS Autonomous Fix Loop. All repo-specific behaviour is isolated in a per-repo adapter; core orchestrator and agents never contain repo-specific logic. Onboarding a new repo means pointing the introspection agent at it - it reads the repo, generates the adapter and manifest, the user approves via email, and the harness goes live with zero core changes. See Harness Extensibility spec (May 2026 Draft).

userStories.title: Shared LLM client via OpenRouter
userStories.description: As every AI-backed agent in the harness (triage classifier, PM agent, fix generator, build scaffolder, introspection draft generator, eval reasoning, docs agent, usability agent), I need one shared LLM client that talks to OpenRouter so the operator can swap models per agent without code changes. OpenRouter exposes an OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint and supports Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, Llama, Mistral and others through a single API key. Today the codebase has no model wired up - every agent has an LLM-shaped interface (TriageClassifier, FixGenerator, ScaffoldGenerator, etc.) backed only by deterministic heuristic stubs. This story replaces those stubs with a real provider while keeping the interfaces so existing tests can still inject fakes.
acceptanceCriteria: core/llm/client.ts exports an LLMClient class with chat(messages, options?) and chatJson<T>(messages, schema, options?) methods; chatJson validates the response against a JSON schema and retries on parse failure (default 3 retries)
Client posts to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions with the OpenAI-compatible request shape and an HTTP-Referer + X-Title header per OpenRouter conventions
Authentication reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY from env on construction and fails fast with a clear error if missing

Per-agent model selection via env: OPENROUTER_MODEL_TRIAGE, OPENROUTER_MODEL_PM, OPENROUTER_MODEL_FIX, OPENROUTER_MODEL_BUILD, OPENROUTER_MODEL_EVAL, OPENROUTER_MODEL_DOCS, OPENROUTER_MODEL_USABILITY, OPENROUTER_MODEL_INTROSPECTION; each falls back to OPENROUTER_MODEL_DEFAULT (e.g. anthropic/claude-3.7-sonnet) when unset
Streaming is not required for v1; non-streaming JSON responses only

Token usage from the response (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens) is returned alongside the content and forwarded to the existing cost-guardrails RunUsage accumulator so per-run token caps actually fire on real usage
Built-in retry with exponential backoff for 429/5xx (default max 5 attempts, jittered), and timeout (default 90s) configurable per call

Real LLM-backed implementations replace the heuristic stubs in: TriageClassifier (HeuristicClassifier becomes a fallback used only when OPENROUTER_API_KEY is absent), FixGenerator (no current real impl), ScaffoldGenerator (no current real impl), and the new introspection generator from US-105; the existing interfaces are preserved so test fakes still work
All test suites continue to pass with mocked clients; a thin LLMClient mock helper is exported from core/llm/test-utils for reuse

Unit tests cover: missing API key error, per-agent model resolution from env with default fallback, retry/backoff on 429, JSON parse retry on malformed response, token usage forwarded to cost guardrails, request body matches OpenAI chat-completions schema, custom headers (HTTP-Referer, X-Title) included
README documents the env vars and links to the OpenRouter model list so the operator can swap models per agent without redeploying
userStories.notes: Implemented shared OpenRouter-backed LLMClient (src/llm-client.ts) with chat() + chatJson() (AJV schema validation + parse retries), retry/backoff for 429/5xx, timeout, token usage capture + onUsage callback, and OpenRouter headers. Added OpenRouter-backed implementations for TriageClassifier/FixGenerator/ScaffoldGenerator plus MockLLMClient helper and Jest unit tests. Added README env var documentation. (Note: files placed under src/ due to environment directory-creation constraints; will move to core/llm/ during US-102 restructure.)

userStories.title: Adapter interface contract
userStories.description: As a harness developer, I need a single TypeScript interface that every per-repo adapter implements so core agents have one stable contract for repo-specific behaviour. The interface defines five methods - classifyModule, getTestCommands, getSandboxServices, runCustomEval, getPRMetadata - and the supporting types Issue, ServiceConfig, SandboxOutput, EvalResult, and PRMetadata.
acceptanceCriteria: core/adapter.interface.ts exports the RepoAdapter interface with exactly the five methods specified in spec section 2
All five methods are async (return Promise) so adapters can perform network I/O without interface changes
Supporting types ServiceConfig, SandboxOutput, EvalResult, PRMetadata, and Issue are exported with the field shapes from spec section 2.1
Interface and supporting types include TSDoc comments derived from the spec describing when each method is called and by which agent
A base adapter class (or default-implementation helper) is provided so adapters can return safe defaults (empty array, empty object, passed: true) without overriding every method
Type-level test (tsc --noEmit on a fixture) verifies a minimal adapter implementation compiles against the interface
Unit tests verify the supporting types serialize/deserialize correctly when a SandboxOutput is round-tripped through JSON
userStories.notes: Added core/adapter.interface.ts as the contract source-of-truth (RepoAdapter + Issue/ServiceConfig/SandboxOutput/EvalResult/PRMetadata, plus BaseRepoAdapter defaults) with TSDoc. Kept src/adapter.interface.ts as a re-export shim for existing imports. Added a tsc compile fixture (src/adapter.interface.fixture.ts) and unit tests validating JSON round-trip and BaseRepoAdapter default eval. Verified with: npm test -- src/adapter.interface.test.ts.

userStories.title: Harness directory restructure with one-way dependency
userStories.description: As a harness developer, I need the existing flat src/ layout reorganised into core/ and configs/ with a strict one-way import rule so repo-specific code can never leak into core. The current code lives flat in src/ (60+ files: triage-agent.ts, pm-agent.ts, fix-agent.ts, build-agent.ts, eval-agent.ts, sandbox-runner.ts, fork-manager.ts, gmail-mcp.ts, pm-email-loop.ts, retry-loop.ts, regression-guard.ts, usability-agent.ts, docs-agent.ts, issue-sweep.ts, multi-repo.ts, cost-guardrails.ts, plus their *-types/-index/.test files) with three subfolders src/manifest/, src/orchestrator/, src/webhook/. core/ never imports from configs/; configs/ may import from core/.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository layout matches spec section 1: core/orchestrator.ts (current src/orchestrator/state-machine.ts entrypoint), core/agents/{triage,pm,fix,build,eval,docs,usability,regression-guard,introspection}.ts, core/email.ts (re-exports from gmail-mcp + pm-email-loop), core/github.ts (re-exports from fork-manager), core/sandbox.ts (current sandbox-runner.ts), core/manifest/, core/webhook/, core/adapter.interface.ts (new), configs/<org>/<repo>/{manifest.yaml,adapter.ts}, .github/workflows/sandbox.yml, scripts/

All existing src/*.ts files are relocated into core/ subfolders by responsibility (agents -> core/agents/, infra -> core/, manifest/orchestrator/webhook keep their subfolder structure under core/) with imports updated; no behavioural changes
examples/openinference.yaml is moved to configs/arize-ai/openinference/manifest.yaml as part of US-111; the old examples/openinference.yaml is deleted
An automated lint check fails the build if any file under core/ imports from configs/ (AST-based check via tsc resolution or eslint-plugin-import rule, run as part of npm run lint)
Files under configs/ may import from core/adapter.interface.ts and core/* type modules; this direction is permitted by the lint rule
tsconfig.json rootDir is updated from ./src to a path that covers both core/ and configs/, and jest.config.ts roots include both
README updated to describe the directory contract and the one-way dependency rule

All existing unit tests (manifest, orchestrator, webhook, every agent, retry-loop, sandbox-runner, multi-repo, cost-guardrails, fork-manager, gmail-mcp, pm-email-loop, issue-sweep) pass after the restructure with no test logic changes other than import paths

userStories.title: Manifest adapter-aware refactor
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need an updated manifest schema that contains only configuration values - no executable logic. The existing src/manifest/types.ts Manifest interface currently has test_command (string), sandbox_services (string[]), issue_types (array), and skip_pm_gate (boolean) - all four are removed or replaced because executable logic now lives in the adapter and skip_pm_gate becomes a per-issue label. New fields skip_pm_gate_label and sandbox_timeout_mins are added. Unknown fields fail startup loudly. This refactor coexists with the existing schema_version field used by multi-repo-types.ts (v1/v2 multi-repo coordination) - they are orthogonal and both must remain valid.
acceptanceCriteria: core/manifest schema covers exactly the fields in spec section 3: repo, trigger_label, skip_pm_gate_label, fork_org, branch_prefix, pm_email, approval_keywords, max_retries, sandbox_timeout_mins (plus the orthogonal schema_version and coordinated_repos fields preserved from multi-repo-types.ts)

Required fields: repo, fork_org, pm_email; all other fields have defaults (trigger_label=agent-fix, skip_pm_gate_label=trivial-fix, branch_prefix=agent/scope-, approval_keywords=[approved, lgtm, ship it], max_retries=3, sandbox_timeout_mins=15)

Removed from Manifest: test_command, sandbox_services, issue_types, skip_pm_gate (boolean). Their presence in a manifest is rejected with a clear migration hint: test_command/sandbox_services -> adapter.getTestCommands/getSandboxServices, issue_types -> no longer needed (triage emits the type), skip_pm_gate -> use the skip_pm_gate_label on the issue instead
Schema rejects unknown fields - additionalProperties=false at every object level - and the orchestrator fails startup with a clear field-level error pointing at the offending key

Loader applies defaults, returns a fully-populated typed Manifest object, and exposes a migration helper migrateLegacyManifest(input) that strips test_command/sandbox_services/issue_types/skip_pm_gate, sets skip_pm_gate_label=trivial-fix when skip_pm_gate was true, and emits a warning per stripped field

All call sites that previously read manifest.test_command, manifest.sandbox_services, manifest.issue_types, manifest.skip_pm_gate (currently in webhook/router.ts, triage-agent.ts, sandbox-runner.ts, multi-repo.ts) are updated to read from the adapter or from the new fields; tsc passes

src/manifest/__tests__/loader.test.ts is updated for the new shape (existing tests for those four fields are migrated, not deleted) plus new tests for: skip_pm_gate_label default, sandbox_timeout_mins default, removed-field rejection with migration hint, migrateLegacyManifest behaviour, coexistence with schema_version=2 + coordinated_repos

userStories.title: Repo signal gathering
userStories.description: As the introspection agent, I need a function that shallow-clones a repo and extracts the structured signals needed to generate an adapter draft: CI workflows, package manifests, Makefile/justfile targets, CONTRIBUTING/DEVELOPMENT docs, docker-compose services, and README content.
acceptanceCriteria: gatherRepoSignals(repoFullName) performs a shallow git clone (depth=1) into a temp directory and cleans up on completion or error
Reads .github/workflows/*.yml and extracts test job step commands verbatim into signals.ciWorkflows
Reads package.json, pyproject.toml, setup.py, Cargo.toml, go.mod, pom.xml, build.gradle if present and records language stack and configured test runner into signals.packageManifests
Reads Makefile and justfile if present and extracts targets matching test/check/verify into signals.makefileTargets
Reads CONTRIBUTING.md and DEVELOPMENT.md if present and extracts code blocks into signals.contributingDocs
Reads docker-compose.yml/compose.yaml if present and extracts service entries into signals.composeServices
Reads README.md as fallback into signals.readme (only used when nothing else yielded test commands)
Detects monorepo layout when multiple package manifests exist in subdirectories and records subdirectory->stack mapping
Function is tolerant of missing files - any unread signal is an empty array/string, never an exception
Unit tests use fixture repos covering: pure Node monorepo, single-package Python repo, repo with only Makefile, repo with only README, repo with no test signals at all

userStories.title: Draft adapter generator (LLM prompt via OpenRouter)
userStories.description: As the introspection agent, I need a function generateDraftAdapter(signals, repoFullName) that calls the shared LLMClient (US-100, model selected by OPENROUTER_MODEL_INTROSPECTION) with the full RepoAdapter interface source plus the gathered signals and returns a draft adapter.ts, manifest.yaml, decision rationale, and a list of open items. Function is deterministic given identical signals + model + temperature=0; the LLM call is the only source of non-determinism.
acceptanceCriteria: generateDraftAdapter calls LLMClient.chatJson with temperature=0 and a JSON schema covering {adapterTs, manifestYaml, rationale, openItems}; the LLM call is the only source of non-determinism
The prompt includes the full current contents of core/adapter.interface.ts at runtime (not a cached or hard-coded copy) so generated adapters always match the current interface version

The prompt instructs the model to: implement all five interface methods, prefer CI workflow steps for getTestCommands, prefer compose services for getSandboxServices, generate keyword-matching classifyModule appropriate to the repo layout, implement runCustomEval to check exit codes plus coverage when configured, and export a default class named after the repo
Returned JSON has keys adapterTs (string), manifestYaml (string), rationale (Record<string,string> mapping decision to source signal), openItems (string[])
Malformed JSON triggers automatic retry with the same prompt via LLMClient.chatJson's built-in retry (default 3 attempts) before failing
Generated adapter.ts compiles against core/adapter.interface.ts (verified by tsc --noEmit on the temp file before continuing)
Generated adapter.ts always uses default export (export default class ...) - validated by parsing the output before sending the email
Token usage from the call is forwarded to the run's cost-guardrails RunUsage accumulator (via US-100)
Unit tests with a mocked LLMClient cover: monorepo with multiple packages, single-package repo, repo with only README signals, malformed JSON retry path, interface-mismatch failure path

userStories.title: Introspection email review loop
userStories.description: As the introspection agent, I need a multi-turn email conversation that sends the draft adapter to pm_email, waits for a reply, and either activates on approval or revises the draft using the user's corrections. The loop reuses the same Gmail MCP send + watch infrastructure as the PM agent design conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: sendIntrospectionEmail(pmEmail, draft) sends an email containing: full manifest.yaml as a code block, full adapter.ts as a code block, rationale section explaining each decision and its source signal, open items list, and instructions to reply approve or with corrections
waitForEmailReply(repoFullName) blocks until a reply arrives on the same thread (or a configurable per-repo introspection thread)
isApproval(body) returns true when the body contains any of the configured approval_keywords (default approved/lgtm/ship it), case-insensitive, ignoring quoted reply text

On non-approval reply, reviseDraft(current, replyBody, signals) calls the shared LLMClient (US-100) with the previous draft, the reply, and the original signals and produces a revised draft; the loop sends the revised draft and waits again
Loop has a configurable max iterations (default 10) before failing with a clear error so a stuck conversation cannot run forever
Loop is restart-safe - the in-flight introspection state is persisted (repo, last draft, iteration count) and resumes after orchestrator restart
Unit tests with mocked email and LLMClient cover: first-reply approval, two-revision-then-approval, max-iterations exceeded, persisted-state resume, quoted-text approval-keyword false-positive guard

userStories.title: Introspection orchestration and activation
userStories.description: As the introspection agent, after approval I need to write the generated files into configs/<org>/<repo>/, add the four required labels (agent-fix, trivial-fix, agent-failed, needs-design) to the upstream repo, log activation, and re-queue the issue event that triggered introspection so the normal pipeline takes over.
acceptanceCriteria: runIntrospection(repoFullName, pmEmail, forkOrg) executes the full flow: gatherRepoSignals -> generateDraftAdapter -> email loop -> writeAdapterFiles -> addGitHubLabels -> log activation

writeAdapterFiles creates configs/<org>/<repo>/manifest.yaml and configs/<org>/<repo>/adapter.ts with the approved content; the directory is created if missing; existing files are not overwritten without an explicit force flag
addGitHubLabels is idempotent: creates each of the four labels (agent-fix, trivial-fix, agent-failed, needs-design) on the upstream repo, skipping any that already exist
runIntrospection blocks the orchestrator pipeline for this repo until approval; concurrent issue events for the same repo queue up rather than running in parallel

After successful activation, the original issue event that triggered introspection is re-queued and processed by the normal pipeline (loadAdapter now succeeds because configs/<org>/<repo>/adapter.ts exists)

On any failure (signal gathering, LLM error after retries, file-write error, label-creation error) the run transitions to FAILED with a structured reason, an error email is sent to pm_email, and the original issue event is NOT marked as processed so it can be re-tried after the operator fixes the problem
Unit tests cover: full happy path, partial-failure rollback (file written but labels failed), concurrent-event queueing, re-queue verification, idempotent label creation

userStories.title: Adapter loader with auto-introspection trigger
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, when handling an issue event I need to dynamically load the per-repo adapter from configs/<org>/<repo>/adapter.ts, and if no adapter exists I need to run introspection automatically before continuing. Adapters export a default class; the orchestrator instantiates it via new mod.default().
acceptanceCriteria: loadAdapter(repoFullName) resolves the path configs/<org>/<repo>/adapter.ts and dynamically imports it
If the adapter file does not exist, loadAdapter calls runIntrospection(repoFullName, DEFAULT_PM_EMAIL, DEFAULT_FORK_ORG) and waits for it to complete before retrying the import
DEFAULT_PM_EMAIL and DEFAULT_FORK_ORG are read from environment variables; missing values raise a clear startup error rather than triggering introspection with undefined
Adapter import expects a default export; missing or non-class default export raises a typed AdapterContractError with the offending repo path
Loaded adapter is instantiated as new mod.default() and the result is verified at runtime to satisfy the RepoAdapter shape (all five methods present and callable)
handleIssueEvent integrates: loadManifest -> loadAdapter -> runPipeline({event, manifest, adapter}); adapter is passed through to all downstream agents

Unit tests cover: existing adapter loaded successfully, missing adapter triggers introspection then loads, missing default export raises AdapterContractError, missing env vars raise startup error, runtime shape check rejects an adapter missing a method

userStories.title: Wire adapter into core agents (incl. SandboxOutput shape change)
userStories.description: As a core agent author, I need triage, sandbox runner, eval, and PR creator to call the adapter methods exclusively for repo-specific behaviour. This includes a breaking type change: the existing SandboxArtifact (single exitCode/stdout/stderr) is replaced or wrapped by the spec's SandboxOutput (array of per-command {command, exitCode, stdout, stderr}) so adapter.runCustomEval() can attribute results per command. The HeuristicClassifier in triage-agent.ts (label/keyword classification + path-matching identifyModule) is removed from core and replaced with adapter.classifyModule().
acceptanceCriteria: Triage agent calls adapter.classifyModule(issue) to obtain the affected module path; HeuristicClassifier and identifyModule logic are deleted from core/agents/triage.ts (the issue-type classification stays - only the module-routing heuristics move to the adapter)

Sandbox runner calls adapter.getTestCommands() and adapter.getSandboxServices() to assemble workflow inputs - sandbox-runner.ts no longer reads manifest.test_command or manifest.sandbox_services and validateSandboxConfig no longer requires testCommand

SandboxArtifact in core/sandbox-types.ts is extended (or replaced) so the artifact contains commands: Array<{command, exitCode, stdout, stderr}> matching the spec's SandboxOutput; existing single-command callers get a synthesised single-element array during migration

Eval agent (eval-agent.ts evaluateSandboxResults) calls adapter.runCustomEval(sandboxOutput) to obtain the structured EvalResult and stops computing overallPass from a single exitCode; the generic exit-code-only eval becomes the default on the base adapter and is removed from core/agents/eval.ts
PR creator calls adapter.getPRMetadata(issues) and merges returned extraLabels and extraBodySections with harness defaults

Multi-repo coordinator (multi-repo.ts) is updated for the new flow - per-repo CoordinatedRepo entries no longer carry test_command (it comes from each repo's adapter); existing multi-repo tests are updated accordingly

classifyModule return value is validated to be a valid directory path relative to the repo root (no leading /, no .., resolves to an existing directory in the cloned fork) before being passed to fix and build agents; invalid paths fail the run with a typed AdapterContractError

Existing unit tests for triage-agent.test.ts (HeuristicClassifier suites), sandbox-runner.test.ts, eval-agent.test.ts, multi-repo.test.ts are migrated to inject a fake adapter; net test count does not decrease; new tests verify each agent calls the adapter with the documented arguments

userStories.title: Parameterised shared sandbox workflow
userStories.description: As the sandbox runner, I need a single shared GitHub Actions workflow that any repo's sandbox run dispatches against. The workflow has no repo-specific knowledge - test commands and services are passed as base64-encoded JSON inputs derived from the adapter output. This replaces the current sandbox-runner.ts buildWorkflowInputs path that passes a single testCommand string and the SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_FILE constant.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/sandbox.yml exists with workflow_dispatch inputs: repo_full_name, fork_clone_url, branch_name, test_commands_b64 (base64 JSON string array), services_b64 (base64 JSON ServiceConfig[] array)

Workflow checks out the fork branch, decodes services_b64, starts each declared service via scripts/start-services.js, then decodes test_commands_b64 and executes each command in sequence via scripts/run-commands.js
scripts/start-services.js spins up declared services using docker run with the configured image and port mapping, and waits on healthCheckUrl when provided (with a timeout) before returning

scripts/run-commands.js runs each command sequentially, halts on the first non-zero exit, and emits a JSON artifact with {command, exitCode, stdout, stderr} per command matching the SandboxOutput shape from US-101

core/sandbox.ts (current sandbox-runner.ts) is updated: buildWorkflowInputs now takes string[] commands + ServiceConfig[] services and emits test_commands_b64 + services_b64 instead of a single testCommand; SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_FILE points at the new sandbox.yml
Sandbox runner serialises adapter.getTestCommands() and adapter.getSandboxServices() to base64 JSON and triggers the workflow via workflow_dispatch
Sandbox runner reads the emitted JSON artifact and returns it as the new SandboxArtifact.commands array (US-109) so eval can consume it via adapter.runCustomEval
Workflow is parameterised only - no secret values come from the adapter; secrets are injected from the GitHub Actions environment using repo_full_name as a namespace prefix
Sandbox wall-time is capped by manifest.sandbox_timeout_mins; runner cancels the workflow run on timeout and records timeout outcome (preserving existing SandboxTimeoutError behaviour)

sandbox-runner.test.ts existing tests are migrated to the new input shape; new tests cover: multi-command serialization, service inclusion, single-command compatibility (single-element array), timeout still triggers SandboxTimeoutError
Integration test dispatches the workflow against a fixture fork repo and verifies the JSON artifact matches the SandboxOutput shape consumed by the eval agent

userStories.title: OpenInference reference adapter and validator script
userStories.description: As the harness reference, I need configs/arize-ai/openinference/{manifest.yaml, adapter.ts} produced via the introspection conversation (not hand-written) and the standalone scripts/validate_spans.py committed in the harness repo. The adapter routes openinference issues across python/js/instrumentation submodules and parses VIOLATION: lines from the span validator output. The current examples/openinference.yaml (which contains the now-removed test_command, sandbox_services, issue_types, skip_pm_gate fields) is deleted as part of this story and its content informs the introspection-generated manifest.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/validate_spans.py is committed in the harness repo and accepts --phoenix-url; emits one VIOLATION: <message> line per failure on stdout and exits non-zero on any violation

configs/arize-ai/openinference/manifest.yaml is generated by the introspection conversation with repo=Arize-ai/openinference, fork_org and pm_email from bootstrap, and defaults for everything else; old examples/openinference.yaml is deleted

configs/arize-ai/openinference/adapter.ts is generated by the introspection conversation, exports default class OpenInferenceAdapter implements RepoAdapter, and matches the reference shape in spec section 6 (classifyModule routes to python/openinference-semantic-conventions, js/packages, python/instrumentation/openinference-instrumentation-<name>, or python/instrumentation default)
getTestCommands returns the pytest, npm test, and validate_spans.py commands in that order; getSandboxServices returns the arizephoenix/phoenix:latest service with the documented healthCheckUrl

runCustomEval finds the validate_spans command in the SandboxOutput, parses VIOLATION: lines, and returns passed=true only when both pytest exitCode is 0 and there are zero violations; retryContext lists the violation messages when present
getPRMetadata returns extraLabels=['openinference']
End-to-end test runs the adapter against a recorded SandboxOutput fixture and verifies pass and fail paths produce the expected EvalResult

userStories.title: Onboarding bootstrap and watched-list trigger
userStories.description: As an operator, I need to onboard a new repo by either adding it to a bootstrap watched list or by labeling an issue with agent-fix on a repo that has no configs/ folder yet. Both paths trigger introspection automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Bootstrap config (file or env) supports a watched_repos list with entries {repo, pm_email, fork_org}; on harness start, any watched repo without configs/<org>/<repo>/ enqueues a runIntrospection job

Webhook handler detects issue.labeled events with the trigger label on a repo that has no configs/<org>/<repo>/adapter.ts and triggers runIntrospection with DEFAULT_PM_EMAIL and DEFAULT_FORK_ORG before processing the event
After introspection completes successfully, the original issue event is re-processed end-to-end through the normal pipeline (verified by the same run-id appearing in pipeline logs after activation)
Operator-facing README documents the five onboarding steps from spec section 8

Integration test simulates the auto-trigger path: webhook receives issue.labeled for a repo with no adapter -> introspection runs against a fixture repo -> approval email simulated -> configs/ written -> labels added -> original issue processed

userStories.title: Harness contract enforcement (constraints lint)
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I need automated checks that enforce the architectural constraints from spec section 9 so the adapter contract is not silently broken in PRs.
acceptanceCriteria: Build fails when any file under core/ contains an import path resolving inside configs/
Build fails when any file under configs/<org>/<repo>/adapter.ts does not have a default class export

Build fails when the RepoAdapter interface is modified without bumping a documented version constant; PR template asks contributors to update every existing adapter or add a default-implementation method on the base class
Manifest validator rejects unknown fields with a clear field-level error and is wired into harness startup
A startup-time check verifies each adapter method on every loaded adapter is async (returns a Promise) and rejects the adapter if any method is synchronous
Runtime check rejects classifyModule return values that contain '..' segments, leading '/', or resolve outside the cloned fork directory
Unit and lint tests cover each enforcement path with both a passing and a failing fixture
